title: leontopodium alpinum subsp. nivale
intro: Leontopodium alpinum subsp. nivale, also known as Alpine Edelweiss, is a subspecies of flowering plant that grows in the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, typically found in high-altitude areas where the air is thin. It is a perennial plant with small white flowers and is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Alpine Edelweiss are said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral activities. In herbal preparations, Alpine Edelweiss is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
